extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r3, whose antecedent is the conjunction of 8 conditions including diabetes. rec_drug_b is set true by r2 whenever diabetes is true. Therefore any input satisfying r3's antecedent necessarily also satisfies r2's antecedent (diabetes=true is required by both), so both rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are set true. No rule sets either output to false, and the default applies only to unset outputs, so neither true assignment is overridden; priorities are irrelevant since there is no competing rule. Setting diabetes, hypertension, peptic_ulcer, pregnancy, qt_prolongation, heart_failure, seizure_history, and hepatic_impairment all true (ckd_dialysis free, set false) makes r3 fire (rec_drug_a=true) and r2 fire (rec_drug_b=true), so rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b holds, violating mutual exclusion.
